title: How the whole app fits together
objective: See the full path a tap takes, from the Android screen to Postgres and back.

body.p: The repo is two separate programs that talk over HTTP: an Android app (Kotlin) and a backend server (Node/TypeScript). Neither can 'see' the other's code — they only communicate through JSON requests.

The phone can't safely hold a database password or business logic that every user would need updated at once. The server holds the source of truth; the phone holds the UI and a local cache for offline use.
When MainActivity appears on screen, it asks MainViewModel to load data. The ViewModel asks UserRepository, which decides whether to use the local Room cache or call the network.

quiz.q: Why doesn't the Android app talk directly to PostgreSQL?
options: The phone would need database credentials, which isn't safe to ship in an app
